title: Cannot use podman auto-update against a container that was created through podman play kube
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Cannot use podman auto-update against a container that was created through podman play kube
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a new Pod with at least 1 container configured to use auto-update and 1 container that doesnt use auto-update

2. podman generate systemd --files --name --new newapp

3. systemctl --user enable ....    (for all systemd files) 

4. Setup and Test auto-update feature

5. podman generate kube newapp > kube.yml

6. Modify image version for container that doesnt use auto-update in kube.yml

7. podman play kube --replace kube.yml

8. Need regenerate the systemd files using podman generate systemd --files --name podman_drpinfo    (cannot use --new on pod created from Kube file)

9. Restart containers using systemd files

10. podman auto-update 

**Describe the results you received:**

Error: registry auto-updating container "2508498e4eda910598580d2e0d93d4a095a34f0fed897081da32d5a7854f2f9a": raw-image name is empty

**Describe the results you expected:**
UNIT                    CONTAINER            IMAGE                                     POLICY      UPDATED
container-newapp.service  2508498e4eda  docker.io/newapp:dev  registry    false

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.2
API Version:  4.0.2
Go Version:   go1.17.7

Built:      Tue Apr 19 06:16:32 2022
OS/Arch:    linux/amd64

```

Basicly the goal here was to have the auto-update feature be executed from a pipeline whenever a new image was built from our code because the image name and tag never change and to use the kube deployment feature to update nginx and mongodb when we are ready to, by updating the YML and running a pipeline

comments.body: this might just need generate to set use the `WithRootFSFromImage` function. I will take a look at this,

Auto update is only supported with units generated with `--new`, so this will not even if this problem is fixed.

@vrothberg has done some improvements for systemd with play kube, maybe he can take a look when he is back.

I do not think the issue here is play kube as it pretty concretely passes the image name down as the `RawImageName` in all normal scenarios. Something must be going wrong when restarting/re-generating the systemd units the second time.
Yes but as long as units are not created with `--new` auto-update will not work for them.

> Auto update is only supported with units generated with `--new`, so this will not even if this problem is fixed.
> 
> @vrothberg has done some improvements for systemd with play kube, maybe he can take a look when he is back.

`podman generate systemd --new` is not supported for pods created via play-kube. That's explicitly mentioned in the docs (see https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/source/markdown/podman-generate-systemd.1.md#--new).

Trying `--new` on such a pod, I get the following error:
```
~ $ podman generate systemd --new 338
Error: cannot use --new on pod "338dd0aee57b723f72216b81317305582ed207868cf9e259c9bfcd101152d9b4": no create command found
```

Step 8) mentions it doesn't work which is good.  But auto-update requires --new. There's no way around that yet.
Do we record the path to the YAML file somewhere.  Could we at least give the users some indicator on how to launch a YAML file with podman generate systemd ?

> Do we record the path to the YAML file somewhere. Could we at least give the users some indicator on how to launch a YAML file with podman generate systemd ?

That's a different request.  I need to go back to the "play-kube in systemd" and write docs and a blog on it.

It remains unclear at the moment how auto-updates can be used in conjunction with play-kube-in-systemd
@vrothberg can we close this as it seems this is expected behavior or do you want to take it?

I think we need to keep this open.  Auto updates still don't work with `play kube`.  But, we have the groundwork done with the systemd integration of play-kube.  What it will probably break down to is that entire service will be restarted if one container has a new image.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I think it is working in 4.3.0 with yaml pods created with the systemd service `podman-kube@` according to the status report of the podman auto updater service.

Thanks for coming back to the issue.  I forgot to drop that this is actually working since Podman v4.3, see commit https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/274d34a25a3ed7b69a6e4caec07e845157048c96.

Please open a new issue if it does not work as expected.
